provenance.source_corpus: Use of Wood Waste in Rehabilitation of Landings Constructed on Fine-Textured Soils, Central Interior British Columbia, Canada

source_spans.text: After 4 years, soil bulk density at 7–14 cm depth was lowest in the chip incorporation treatment.
After 4 years, soil bulk density at 7–14 cm depth was lowest in the chip incorporation treatment.

Although total C, N, and S, and mineralizable N concentrations were highest in the topsoil recovery treatment, the chip incorporation treatment had the highest 3-year growth rates of hybrid white spruce (Picea glauca /H11003engelmannii).

nodes.name: total C, N, and S, and mineralizable N concentrations

source_spans.text: Although total C, N, and S, and mineralizable N concentrations were highest in the topsoil recovery treatment, the chip incorporation treatment had the highest 3-year growth rates of hybrid white spruce (Picea glauca /H11003engelmannii).

nodes.name: 3-year growth rates of hybrid white spruce

source_spans.text: Although total C, N, and S, and mineralizable N concentrations were highest in the topsoil recovery treatment, the chip incorporation treatment had the highest 3-year growth rates of hybrid white spruce (Picea glauca /H11003engelmannii).
The supplementary N fertilizer added with the chips appears to have offset N immobilization by the decomposing wood waste, and maintained satisfactory foliar N concentrations.
The supplementary N fertilizer added with the chips appears to have offset N immobilization by the decomposing wood waste, and maintained satisfactory foliar N concentrations.
Establishment of paper birch ( Betula papyrifera) did not succeed due to severe rodent damage to seedlings, perhaps encouraged by rapid and dense revegetation by seeded agronomic legumes.
